findings.title: Hardcoded absolute paths in validation shell script
findings.description: The file .code-analysis/validation/run-comparison.sh contains two hardcoded absolute paths on lines 4 and 6: PLUGIN_PATH="/Users/keven.foster/..." and cd /Users/keven.foster/.... These paths are bound to a specific developer machine and break portability. Per plugin security step P2, hardcoded absolute paths that should use ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} (or a dynamic equivalent) are a high-severity portability and security risk — they can expose environment topology (username, directory layout) in a distributed plugin artifact.
findings.recommendation: Replace the hardcoded PLUGIN_PATH with a dynamic computation: PLUGIN_PATH="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/../.." && pwd)". Remove the bare 'cd /Users/...' on line 6 and derive the working directory from PLUGIN_PATH instead.

findings.title: Author email exposed in plugin.json
findings.description: The plugin.json manifest includes the author email 'dev@vooban.com' in plain text. While this is a team email rather than a personal one, it is distributed to every machine that installs the plugin and could be harvested for spam or social engineering.
findings.recommendation: Remove the email field from plugin.json or replace it with a URL to a contact page. The author name alone is sufficient for attribution.
